Subject: escalation - recurring CRM data-quality complaints from the field

I'm writing this up on behalf of Customer Success. Over the past few sprints our
account managers have logged a run of small but genuine mistakes in how the contact
tooling saves and shows two fields: a contact's mobile number and their loan type.
Every one of these has been reproduced on a live account, so please treat them as
real defects rather than user error. They only bite when a contact's details come in
a slightly off-pattern shape, which is why they slipped past everyone for weeks - the
routine cases (a plain US mobile, a loan type chosen from the dropdown) come out
perfectly correct, and our automated checks only ever exercise those routine cases.

Here is what the managers actually reported, in their words:

- "I onboarded a borrower in London and typed their mobile the way it's printed on
  their letterhead. Our workspace is configured to default to the UK, but the number
  saved under a US code (+1...) instead of the UK code (+44...)." So: when the digits
  could belong to more than one country, the contact's configured home region is being
  ignored and the wrong country is winning.

- "A few contacts gave me their number in the international style that starts with a
  double-zero access code - a 0044 UK number, for instance. Instead of accepting it,
  the tool told me the number was invalid and I had to retype it by hand." So: the 00
  international prefix isn't being treated as equivalent to a leading +.

- "One of my older accounts is tagged with a loan type from before we cleaned up the
  catalog, and the label on the record printed as 'Hardmoney' - no space - when it
  should read 'Hard Money', same word-by-word capitalization as every other loan type
  we display." So: an unfamiliar loan-type code isn't being turned into a readable,
  properly spaced and capitalized label.

- "A rep entered a loan type as 'Working_Capital', with the odd capitals and the
  underscore, and that's exactly the string that got stored on the contact - not the
  tidy internal code the rest of the system matches against." So: an oddly-cased entry
  of a known type is being stored verbatim rather than normalized to its standard code.

There may be more corners like these; those are simply the ones we could reproduce.
The common thread is that all of it is pure string-and-lookup work - deciding a
country for a number, reshaping a dialed number into storage form, and mapping a
loan-type string to its code and its display label. No database or network calls are
involved, so each case is reproducible from a direct call.

What I need from engineering: make the saved and displayed values correct for these
unusual-but-legitimate inputs, and do it without disturbing how the ordinary inputs
(the ones our current checks cover) already behave. Please don't touch the existing
test files, and confirm the current suite still passes when you're done:

    cd /app/loangen-agent && python -m pytest tests -v

1: """
2: Canonical US SMB loan types for CRM contacts and invite flows.
3: 
4: Each entry has a stable ``id`` (stored in MongoDB) and a human ``label``.
5: """
6: 
7: from __future__ import annotations
8: 
9: import re
10: from typing import List, Optional
11: 
12: LOAN_TYPE_CHOICES: List[dict[str, str]] = [
13:     {"id": "sba7a", "label": "SBA 7(a) Loan"},
14:     {"id": "sba504", "label": "SBA 504 Loan"},
15:     {"id": "sba_express", "label": "SBA Express Loan"},
16:     {"id": "sba_caplines", "label": "SBA CAPlines"},
17:     {"id": "microloan", "label": "SBA Microloan"},
18:     {"id": "term", "label": "Term Loan"},
19:     {"id": "loc", "label": "Line of Credit"},
20:     {"id": "equipment", "label": "Equipment Financing"},
21:     {"id": "working_capital", "label": "Working Capital Loan"},
22:     {"id": "cre", "label": "Commercial Real Estate Loan"},
23:     {"id": "construction", "label": "Construction Loan"},
24:     {"id": "bridge", "label": "Bridge Loan"},
25:     {"id": "acquisition", "label": "Business Acquisition Loan"},
26:     {"id": "franchise", "label": "Franchise Loan"},
27:     {"id": "invoice_factoring", "label": "Invoice Factoring"},
28:     {"id": "mca", "label": "Merchant Cash Advance"},
29:     {"id": "rbf", "label": "Revenue-Based Financing"},
30:     {"id": "abl", "label": "Asset-Based Lending"},
31:     {"id": "startup", "label": "Startup Loan"},
32:     {"id": "usda", "label": "USDA Business & Industry Loan"},
33: ]
34: 
35: _LOAN_TYPE_IDS = {c["id"] for c in LOAN_TYPE_CHOICES}
36: 
37: # id + normalized label + common aliases → canonical id
38: _EXTRA_ALIASES: dict[str, str] = {
39:     "sba 7a": "sba7a",
40:     "sba 7(a)": "sba7a",
41:     "sba-7a": "sba7a",
42:     "sba_7a": "sba7a",
43:     "sba 504": "sba504",
44:     "sba-504": "sba504",
45:     "sba_504": "sba504",
46:     "sba express": "sba_express",
47:     "sba caplines": "sba_caplines",
48:     "caplines": "sba_caplines",
49:     "microloan": "microloan",
50:     "sba microloan": "microloan",
51:     "term loan": "term",
52:     "termloan": "term",
53:     "term_loan": "term",
54:     "line of credit": "loc",
55:     "lineofcredit": "loc",
56:     "line_of_credit": "loc",
57:     "equipment financing": "equipment",
58:     "equipment_financing": "equipment",
59:     "working capital": "working_capital",
60:     "working capital loan": "working_capital",
61:     "working_capital": "working_capital",
62:     "commercial real estate": "cre",
63:     "commercial real estate loan": "cre",
64:     "cre": "cre",
65:     "construction loan": "construction",
66:     "bridge loan": "bridge",
67:     "business acquisition": "acquisition",
68:     "business acquisition loan": "acquisition",
69:     "franchise loan": "franchise",
70:     "invoice factoring": "invoice_factoring",
71:     "merchant cash advance": "mca",
72:     "mca": "mca",
73:     "revenue based financing": "rbf",
74:     "revenue-based financing": "rbf",
75:     "asset based lending": "abl",
76:     "asset-based lending": "abl",
77:     "startup loan": "startup",
78:     "usda": "usda",
79:     "usda business": "usda",
80:     "usda b&i": "usda",
81: }
82: 
83: 
84: def _normalize_key(value: str) -> str:
85:     return re.sub(r"[_\-]+", " ", value.strip().lower()).strip()
86: 
87: 
88: def loan_type_label(loan_type_id: Optional[str]) -> str:
89:     if not loan_type_id:
90:         return ""
91:     for choice in LOAN_TYPE_CHOICES:
92:         if choice["id"] == loan_type_id:
93:             return choice["label"]
94:     return loan_type_id.replace("_", "").title()
95: 
96: 
97: def resolve_loan_type(raw: str) -> Optional[str]:
98:     """Map a CSV/UI loan type string to a canonical id, or None if unrecognized."""
99:     value = (raw or "").strip()
100:     if not value:
101:         return None
102:     compact = value.lower().replace(" ", "").replace("-", "").replace("_", "")
103:     if compact in _LOAN_TYPE_IDS:
104:         return compact
105:     if value.lower() in _LOAN_TYPE_IDS:
106:         return value
107:     normalized = _normalize_key(value)
108:     if normalized in _EXTRA_ALIASES:
109:         return _EXTRA_ALIASES[normalized]
110:     for choice in LOAN_TYPE_CHOICES:
111:         if _normalize_key(choice["label"]) == normalized:
112:             return choice["id"]
113:         if _normalize_key(choice["id"]) == normalized:
114:             return choice["id"]
115:     return None
116: 
117: 
118: def is_valid_loan_type_id(loan_type_id: Optional[str]) -> bool:
119:     return bool(loan_type_id and loan_type_id in _LOAN_TYPE_IDS)

1: """
2: E.164 phone normalization for Cartesia outbound calls.
3: 
4: Numbers may be stored with or without a + country code. When omitted, the number
5: is parsed using CARTESIA_DEFAULT_PHONE_REGION (default: US).
6: """
7: 
8: from __future__ import annotations
9: 
10: import re
11: from typing import Optional
12: 
13: import phonenumbers
14: from phonenumbers import NumberParseException, PhoneNumberFormat
15: 
16: _NON_DIGIT_RE = re.compile(r"[^\d+]+")
17: _FALLBACK_REGION = "US"
18: # Regions tried when no + country code is provided (default region first).
19: _CRM_FALLBACK_REGIONS = ("US", "CA", "IN", "GB", "AU", "MX", "DE", "FR")
20: 
21: 
22: def _format_parsed_number(parsed: phonenumbers.PhoneNumber, *, require_valid: bool) -> Optional[str]:
23:     if require_valid:
24:         if not phonenumbers.is_valid_number(parsed):
25:             return None
26:     elif not phonenumbers.is_possible_number(parsed):
27:         return None
28:     return phonenumbers.format_number(parsed, PhoneNumberFormat.E164)
29: 
30: 
31: def _try_parse_candidate(
32:     candidate: str,
33:     region: Optional[str],
34:     *,
35:     require_valid: bool,
36: ) -> Optional[str]:
37:     try:
38:         parsed = phonenumbers.parse(candidate, region)
39:     except NumberParseException:
40:         return None
41:     return _format_parsed_number(parsed, require_valid=require_valid)
42: 
43: 
44: def try_normalize_phone_to_e164(
45:     raw: str,
46:     *,
47:     default_region: Optional[str] = None,
48: ) -> Optional[str]:
49:     """
50:     Best-effort E.164 normalization for CRM storage.
51: 
52:     Accepts international numbers with or without a + prefix. Tries the configured
53:     default region first, then common regions, preferring valid parses over
54:     possible-only parses. Falls back to a +digits form for 8–15 digit inputs.

1: """
2: Beanie ODM model for SMB CRM contacts.
3: 
4: Collection:
5:     smb_contacts — contacts imported or manually added by a lender's CRM.
6: 
7: Every contact is scoped to a single lender via lender_id.
8: A (lender_id, email) compound unique index prevents duplicate contacts
9: within the same lender account while allowing the same email across
10: different lenders.
11: 
12: Status lifecycle:
13:     NEW → CONTACTED → QUALIFIED → CONVERTED / DISQUALIFIED
14: """
15: 
16: from __future__ import annotations
17: 
18: from datetime import datetime, timezone
19: from typing import List, Optional
20: 
21: from beanie import Document
22: from pymongo import ASCENDING, IndexModel
23: from pydantic import Field
24: 
25: 
26: class SMBContact(Document):
27:     """A single CRM contact belonging to a lender."""
28: 
29:     # Owner
30:     lender_id: str  # str(Lender.id)
31: 
32:     # Identity
33:     first_name: str
34:     last_name: str = ""
35:     email: str
36:     mobile: str = ""  # E.164 when set; may be empty until lender adds via Edit
37: 
38:     # Location — optional
39:     address: Optional[str] = None
40:     city: Optional[str] = None
41:     state: Optional[str] = None
42:     zip: Optional[str] = None
43: 
44:     # Business — optional
45:     business_name: Optional[str] = None
46:     industry: Optional[str] = None
47: 
48:     # Qualification hints (optional, from CSV or manual entry)
49:     monthly_revenue: Optional[str] = None
50:     time_in_business: Optional[str] = None
51:     loan_type: Optional[str] = None  # canonical id from loan_types.LOAN_TYPE_CHOICES
52:     loan_amount: Optional[int] = None  # USD whole dollars; from SMB invite or lender edit
53:     comment: Optional[str] = None  # lender notes; not imported from CSV
54: 
55:     # Categorisation
56:     tags: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
57: 
58:     # How the contact entered the CRM
59:     source: str = "manual"  # csv_upload | manual | quickbooks | referral | inbound
60: 
61:     # Status within the lender's CRM pipeline
62:     status: str = "NEW"
63: 
64:     created_at: datetime = Field(
65:         default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc)
66:     )
67:     updated_at: Optional[datetime] = None
68: 
69:     class Settings:
70:         name = "smb_contacts"
71:         indexes = [
72:             # Unique contact per lender (email-scoped)
73:             IndexModel(
74:                 [("lender_id", ASCENDING), ("email", ASCENDING)],
75:                 unique=True,
76:                 name="idx_smb_contacts_lender_email_unique",
77:             ),
78:             IndexModel(
79:                 [("lender_id", ASCENDING)],
80:                 name="idx_smb_contacts_lender_id",
81:             ),
82:             IndexModel(
83:                 [("status", ASCENDING)],
84:                 name="idx_smb_contacts_status",
85:             ),
86:         ]
87: 
88:     def __repr__(self) -> str:
89:         return (
90:             f"<SMBContact id={self.id} email={self.email} "
91:             f"lender_id={self.lender_id}>"
92:         )

Free resources at NFCC.org.\n\n**Expected Timeline:** 12-18 months minimum. Bankruptcy stays on report 7-10 years but its impact fades. """Tests for SMB CRM contact enrichment: CSV import, PATCH, phone normalization, loan types."""

from __future__ import annotations

import unittest
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch

from fastapi import HTTPException

from agent.services.smbcontacts.loan_types import resolve_loan_type
from agent.services.smbcontacts.schemas import CreateSMBContactRequest, UpdateSMBContactRequest
from agent.services.smbcontacts import service as smb_contact_service


class LoanTypeResolverTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_resolves_canonical_id(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(resolve_loan_type("sba7a"), "sba7a")

    def test_resolves_label(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(resolve_loan_type("SBA 7(a) Loan"), "sba7a")

    def test_resolves_alias(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(resolve_loan_type("line of credit"), "loc")

    def test_unknown_returns_none(self) -> None:
        self.assertIsNone(resolve_loan_type("not-a-real-loan"))


class PhoneNormalizationTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_normalize_us_number(self) -> None:
        with patch.object(
            smb_contact_service.settings,
            "cartesia_default_phone_region",
            "US",
        ):
            result = smb_contact_service.normalize_mobile_for_storage("(512) 555-0142")
        self.assertTrue(result.startswith("+1"))

    def test_try_normalize_indian_number_without_country_code(self) -> None:
        with patch.object(
            smb_contact_service.settings,
            "cartesia_default_phone_region",
            "US",
        ):
            result = smb_contact_service.try_normalize_mobile("8050306043")
        self.assertEqual(result, "+918050306043")

    def test_try_normalize_explicit_international(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(
            smb_contact_service.try_normalize_mobile("+918050306043"),
            "+918050306043",
        )

    def test_try_normalize_invalid_returns_none(self) -> None:
        with patch.object(
            smb_contact_service.settings,
            "cartesia_default_phone_region",
            "US",
        ):
            self.assertIsNone(smb_contact_service.try_normalize_mobile("abc"))


class ImportContactsCsvTests(unittest.IsolatedAsyncioTestCase):
    async def test_imports_row_with_optional_last_name_and_warnings(self) -> None:
        csv_text = (
            "first_name,last_name,email,mobile,loan_type,monthly_revenue,time_in_business\n"
            "Jane,,jane@example.com,,bad-type,20k,2 years\n"
            "Bob,Smith,bob@example.com,(512) 555-0199,sba7a,,\n"
        ).encode("utf-8")

        find_chain = MagicMock()
        find_chain.project.return_value.to_list = AsyncMock(return_value=[])

        with patch("agent.services.smbcontacts.service.SMBContact") as contact_cls:
            contact_cls.find.return_value = find_chain
            contact_cls.insert_many = AsyncMock()
            contact_cls.side_effect = lambda **kwargs: MagicMock(**kwargs)
            result = await smb_contact_service.import_contacts_csv(csv_text, lender_id="lender-1")

        self.assertEqual(result.total_rows, 2)
        self.assertEqual(result.imported, 2)
        self.assertEqual(result.skipped, 0)
        self.assertGreaterEqual(result.warnings, 2)
        contact_cls.insert_many.assert_awaited_once()
        inserted = contact_cls.insert_many.await_args.args[0]
        self.assertEqual(len(inserted), 2)

    async def test_skips_row_missing_email(self) -> None:
        csv_text = "first_name,last_name,email,mobile\nJohn,Smith,,555-0100\n".encode("utf-8")

        find_chain = MagicMock()
        find_chain.project.return_value.to_list = AsyncMock(return_value=[])

        with patch("agent.services.smbcontacts.service.SMBContact") as contact_cls:
            contact_cls.find.return_value = find_chain
            contact_cls.insert_many = AsyncMock()
            result = await smb_contact_service.import_contacts_csv(csv_text, lender_id="lender-1")

        self.assertEqual(result.imported, 0)
        self.assertEqual(result.skipped, 1)
        contact_cls.insert_many.assert_not_awaited()

    async def test_updates_existing_contact_mobile_on_duplicate_email(self) -> None:
        csv_text = (
            "first_name,last_name,email,mobile\n"
            "Jim,,lpappajohn@santoracpa.com,3027373362\n"
        ).encode("utf-8")

        existing = MagicMock()
        existing.id = "contact-1"
        existing.email = "lpappajohn@santoracpa.com"
        existing.first_name = "Jim"
        existing.last_name = ""
        existing.mobile = ""
        existing.address = None
        existing.city = None
        existing.state = None
        existing.zip = None
        existing.business_name = "Santora Baffone Group"
        existing.industry = None
        existing.monthly_revenue = None
        existing.time_in_business = None
        existing.loan_type = "mca"
        existing.tags = []
        existing.save = AsyncMock()

        find_chain = MagicMock()
        find_chain.project.return_value.to_list = AsyncMock(return_value=[existing])

        with patch("agent.services.smbcontacts.service.SMBContact") as contact_cls, patch.object(
            smb_contact_service,
            "_locked_contact_ids",
            new_callable=AsyncMock,
            return_value=set(),
        ):
            contact_cls.find.return_value = find_chain
            contact_cls.insert_many = AsyncMock()
            result = await smb_contact_service.import_contacts_csv(csv_text, lender_id="lender-1")

        self.assertEqual(result.imported, 0)
        self.assertEqual(result.updated, 1)
        self.assertEqual(existing.mobile, "+13027373362")
        existing.save.assert_awaited_once()
        contact_cls.insert_many.assert_not_awaited()


class UpdateContactIdentityLockTests(unittest.IsolatedAsyncioTestCase):
    def _mock_contact(self) -> MagicMock:
        contact = MagicMock()
        contact.id = "contact-1"
        contact.lender_id = "lender-1"
        contact.first_name = "Jane"
        contact.last_name = "Doe"
        contact.email = "jane@example.com"
        contact.mobile = "+15125550142"
        contact.source = "manual"
        contact.monthly_revenue = None
        contact.loan_type = None
        contact.loan_amount = None
        contact.comment = None
        contact.tags = []
        contact.address = None
        contact.city = None
        contact.state = None
        contact.zip = None
        contact.business_name = None
        contact.industry = None
        contact.time_in_business = None
        contact.status = "NEW"
        contact.created_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        contact.updated_at = None
        contact.save = AsyncMock()
        return contact

    async def test_blocks_identity_field_when_locked(self) -> None:
        contact = self._mock_contact()

        with patch.object(
            smb_contact_service,
            "_get_lender_contact",
            new_callable=AsyncMock,
            return_value=contact,
        ), patch.object(
            smb_contact_service,
            "_is_identity_locked",
            new_callable=AsyncMock,
            return_value=True,
        ):
            with self.assertRaises(HTTPException) as ctx:
                await smb_contact_service.update_smb_contact(
                    "contact-1",
                    "lender-1",
                    UpdateSMBContactRequest(first_name="Janet"),
                )

        self.assertEqual(ctx.exception.status_code, 400)
        detail = ctx.exception.detail
        assert isinstance(detail, dict)
        self.assertEqual(detail.get("error"), "IDENTITY_LOCKED")
